title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-12
summary: The dominant pattern today is a convergence on AI agent security and autonomous optimization. NVIDIA's SkillSpector, an open-source security scanner for agent skills, gained 822 stars in 24 hours, reflecting developer anxiety about agent safety. Simultaneously, hexo-ai's SIA, a self-improving framework that autonomously optimizes AI systems, added 531 stars, indicating interest in meta-agent loops. Hacker News discussions about a bankrupting agent incident and Anthropic's Fable guardrails reinforce this security-and-autonomy tension. The AI Agent Framework category remains hot with 5 repos averaging 591 daily stars, while Customer Support AI (Chatwoot) shows sustained interest. The absence of new RAG or local LLM launches suggests attention is shifting from knowledge retrieval to agent behavior control.
dataNotes: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first session intelligence and analytics for AI coding agents, with a 100x faster cost tracker than ccusage.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) lack a unified, local-first tool to browse, search, and track costs across all sessions. Existing tools like ccusage are slow and agent-specific.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project has 1,968 stars and gained 540 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and accelerating attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), but the sustained hot trend and recent release suggest consistent organic growth rather than a sudden spike.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) maintain momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a real pain point for the growing number of developers using AI coding agents: unified, local-first analytics and cost tracking. The 100x speed improvement over ccusage is a compelling differentiator.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use multiple AI coding agents, this tool provides a single pane of glass for session history, token usage, and costs, all running locally without accounts.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, privacy-preserving tooling for AI agent workflows. Developers increasingly need observability across multiple agents, and this project fills that gap.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, etc.) who want to track usage, costs, and session history locally.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (1,968 stars) and has 94 open issues, which may indicate growing pains. It relies on local SQLite and may not scale for large teams or enterprise use without PostgreSQL backend.

repository.name: sia
repository.description: SIA is a Self Improving AI framework to autonomously improve the performance of any AI system (Model / Agent) on a benchmark task.

signals: topics 较少，分类信号较弱

hotProjects.whyItMatters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +531，总 stars 1,550，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。

llmSummary.oneLiner: SIA is a self-improving AI framework that autonomously optimizes any AI system on benchmark tasks via a meta-agent loop that updates both the harness and weights.
llmSummary.problemSolved: SIA automates the iterative improvement of AI models and agents on specific tasks, eliminating manual tuning and enabling continuous performance gains without human intervention.
llmSummary.whyNow: SIA has exploded in popularity with 1550 total stars and a 24-hour delta of 531 stars, indicating a massive surge in attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), but the sustained hot trend type and high star velocity suggest strong organic interest. The project is less than a week old (pushed June 11, 2026) and already has 176 forks, signaling rapid adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The initial release and accompanying arXiv paper (2605.27276) likely drove the initial wave, with continued community engagement and recent commits within 7 days maintaining momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: SIA addresses a core pain point in AI development: the need for manual, repetitive fine-tuning. By offering a plug-and-play self-improvement loop with impressive benchmark results (56.6% gain on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels), it captures the imagination of developers seeking to automate optimization. The clean CLI, built-in tasks, and visual dashboard lower the barrier to experimentation.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: SIA provides a ready-to-use framework for automating model/agent improvement on custom tasks. Developers can quickly integrate their own tasks or MLE-Bench competitions and run multi-generation optimization loops with minimal setup.
llmSummary.developerInsight: SIA signals a shift toward meta-learning and automated optimization as a service. For AI developers, this means less time on manual hyperparameter tuning and more focus on task design and evaluation. The framework's ability to improve both the agent's code and weights hints at a future where AI systems continuously self-improve in production.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI researchers, ML engineers, and developers building or fine-tuning models/agents for specific benchmarks or real-world tasks who want to automate performance optimization.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (less than a week old) with no recent releases, so stability and long-term maintenance are unproven. The framework relies on external LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.), which introduces cost and dependency risks. The self-improvement loop may not generalize to all tasks, and the reported benchmark gains may be specific to the tested configurations.

repository.name: SkillSpector
repository.description: Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.

signals: topics 较少，分类信号较弱

hotProjects.whyItMatters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +822，总 stars 3,097，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。

llmSummary.oneLiner: NVIDIA's open-source security scanner that detects vulnerabilities and malicious patterns in AI agent skills before installation.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agent skills (used by Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, etc.) execute with implicit trust and minimal vetting. Research shows 26.1% of skills contain vulnerabilities and 5.2% show likely malicious intent. SkillSpector provides a systematic way to answer 'Is this skill safe to install?'
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 822 stars in the last 24 hours, reaching 3,097 total stars with 232 forks. The acceleration ratio is low (1.0), indicating sustained organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This steady attention reflects growing concern about AI agent security as agentic workflows become mainstream.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and active development suggest ongoing improvements, but no specific release or external mention is evident from the metadata.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As AI agents proliferate (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI), the need for security tooling becomes critical. SkillSpector addresses a clear pain point: vetting third-party skills that can execute arbitrary code. Its comprehensive detection (64 patterns across 16 categories) and support for multiple input formats make it a practical tool for developers.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: SkillSpector is a must-try for anyone building or using AI agent skills. It integrates into CI/CD pipelines via SARIF output and supports LLM-enhanced analysis. The two-stage approach (static + optional LLM) balances speed and depth.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward security-first agent development. Developers should expect skill marketplaces to require scanning, and building with security in mind will become a competitive advantage. The project also highlights the importance of supply chain security for AI workflows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers, security engineers, platform teams integrating agent skills, and any developer using Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, or similar tools.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (no recent release) and may have limited community validation. LLM analysis requires API keys and costs. The scanner's effectiveness depends on pattern coverage, which may miss novel attacks.

modelDemoSignals.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
modelDemoSignals.description: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and so
modelDemoSignals.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li, Jiaying Wu, Yihuai Lan, Shuyue Stella Li, Huichi Zhou, Bowen Jiang, Lei Wang, Jun Wang, Anh Tuan Luu, Caiming Xiong, Hae Won Park, Bryan Hooi, Zhiyuan Hu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
modelDemoSignals.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework
modelDemoSignals.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework that teaches language models to reason by analogy. RA-RFT uses gold-relevance distillation to train a retriever that ranks contexts by expected reasoning benefit rather than semantic overlap, and then fine-tunes the policy model via reinforcement fine-tuning methods with retrieved analogous demonstrations, so the model learns to leverage reasoning traces under verifiable outcome rewards. We further analyze the diversity of retrieved contexts and find that reasoning-aware retrieval surfaces complementary solution strategies that provide distinct reasoning scaffolds for individual problems. Across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks, RA-RFT consistently outperforms standard reinforcement fine-tuning methods. For example, it improves AIME 2025 average@32 accuracy by 7.1 and 2.8 points over GRPO for Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B respectively -- suggesting that reasoning-aware retrieval is a complementary axis of improvement and orthogonal to advances in reward design or training curricula.

modelDemoSignals.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
modelDemoSignals.description: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. modelDemoSignals.summary: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an animation problem. Inspired by computer animation, Mana employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline that transforms procedurally-generated grasp keyframes into manipulation trajectories through motion planning and reinforcement learning. The data generation process is largely automatic, requiring only a few mouse clicks to specify functional affordances (<1 minute per tool). Across four articulated tools spanning different scales and joint types, Mana achieves zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for both grasping and in-hand manipulation, demonstrating a scalable approach to dexterous articulated tool use.
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhao-Heng Yin, Guanya Shi, Pieter Abbeel, C. Karen Liu

modelDemoSignals.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
modelDemoSignals.description: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). modelDemoSignals.summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.

modelDemoSignals.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
modelDemoSignals.description: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the origina
modelDemoSignals.summary: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis. For 7 studies, the LLM could not produce a viable effect size estimate. For the remaining studies, our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a +/-0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases, where conclusions indicate whether the reanalysis supports the original claim. For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases. Together, these results show that LLMs can serve as a scalable tool for automated reproducibility assessment and provide a foundation for systematic auditing of empirical results in the social and behavioral sciences.
modelDemoSignals.author: Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo, Anna Steinberg Schulten, Felix Henninger, Stefan Rose, Sarah Ball, Bolei Ma, Frauke Kreuter, Markus Weinmann, Stefan Feuerriegel
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

aihotHighlights.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
aihotHighlights.summary: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic与IT服务公司DXC Technology达成多年全球联盟。DXC将培训数万名获得Claude认证的前沿部署工程师（FDE），将Claude引入其为全球大型银行、航空公司、保险公司及政府机构运营的关键系统。内部部署中，Claude已成为DXC OASIS平台的默认基础模型，该平台超95%代码由Claude编写，开发速度提升10倍，已服务50多家客户。DXC加入Claude Partner Network，将在保险、现代化服务、网络安全及应用服务四个领域率先推出基于Claude的解决方案。

multiSourceItems.title: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code
multiSourceItems.summary: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code。相比 K2.6，其在 Kimi Code Bench v2 上提升 +21.8%，Program Bench 提升 +11.0%，MLS Bench Lite 提升 +31.5%。推理效率改进，推理 token 使用量降低 30%，长时编码任务中指令遵循和端到端成功率均提升。6x 高速模式即将推出，即日起可通过 Kimi API 和 Kimi Code 使用。

multiSourceItems.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
multiSourceItems.summary: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。

multiSourceItems.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
multiSourceItems.summary: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。

multiSourceItems.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
multiSourceItems.summary: Anthropic与IT服务公司DXC Technology达成多年全球联盟。DXC将培训数万名获得Claude认证的前沿部署工程师（FDE），将Claude引入其为全球大型银行、航空公司、保险公司及政府机构运营的关键系统。内部部署中，Claude已成为DXC OASIS平台的默认基础模型，该平台超95%代码由Claude编写，开发速度提升10倍，已服务50多家客户。DXC加入Claude Partner Network，将在保险、现代化服务、网络安全及应用服务四个领域率先推出基于Claude的解决方案。

multiSourceItems.title: Prometheus 融资120亿美元，估值410亿美元，定位"人工通用工程师"
multiSourceItems.summary: 杰夫·贝佐斯旗下AI公司Prometheus在成立仅7个月、尚无任何产品交付的情况下，以410亿美元估值完成120亿美元融资（最初估值62亿美元）。该公司定位为"人工通用工程师"，目标是将设计到制造的循环压缩10倍以上。但物理经济无法像互联网数据那样抓取，缺乏制造业训练数据。为此，Prometheus计划斥资1000亿美元收购传统工业企业，通过获取工厂生成的数据构建护城河。

multiSourceItems.title: Runway与Lionsgate扩大战略合作
multiSourceItems.summary: 全球内容巨头Lionsgate与生成式AI研究公司Runway今日宣布扩大战略合作。Lionsgate已取得Runway股权，双方将启动联合开发项目，共同创作并制作新IP，首推一部基于Lionsgate现有IP和Runway生成模型的短剧系列。此外，Lionsgate将作为主持伙伴参与6月举行的Runway AI电影节。这是双方继2024年9月首次合作后的深化，Lionsgate此前已在预可视化、故事板及最终帧制作中应用Runway工具。

multiSourceItems.title: Cursor 推出 Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
multiSourceItems.summary: Cursor 近日推出 Auto-review，通过一个专门的分类器智能体在工具调用前审查动作风险。该分类器根据上下文判断动作是否与用户意图一致，高风险时阻止并返回解释给父智能体，低风险时放行。分类器采用小模型，运行在智能体循环内以避免额外延迟，并能读取工作区文件辅助判断。测试基于约12小时内部开发会话生成的6122条标签数据，以及针对读取密钥、操作生产数据等危险场景的合成数据。设计目标是在不频繁阻断日常开发的前提下，拦截风险动作。

multiSourceItems.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
multiSourceItems.description: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and so
multiSourceItems.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.
multiSourceItems.author: Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li, Jiaying Wu, Yihuai Lan, Shuyue Stella Li, Huichi Zhou, Bowen Jiang, Lei Wang, Jun Wang, Anh Tuan Luu, Caiming Xiong, Hae Won Park, Bryan Hooi, Zhiyuan Hu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
multiSourceItems.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework
multiSourceItems.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework that teaches language models to reason by analogy. RA-RFT uses gold-relevance distillation to train a retriever that ranks contexts by expected reasoning benefit rather than semantic overlap, and then fine-tunes the policy model via reinforcement fine-tuning methods with retrieved analogous demonstrations, so the model learns to leverage reasoning traces under verifiable outcome rewards. We further analyze the diversity of retrieved contexts and find that reasoning-aware retrieval surfaces complementary solution strategies that provide distinct reasoning scaffolds for individual problems. Across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks, RA-RFT consistently outperforms standard reinforcement fine-tuning methods. For example, it improves AIME 2025 average@32 accuracy by 7.1 and 2.8 points over GRPO for Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B respectively -- suggesting that reasoning-aware retrieval is a complementary axis of improvement and orthogonal to advances in reward design or training curricula.
multiSourceItems.author: Zilin Xiao, Qi Ma, Chun-cheng Jason Chen, Xintao Chen, Avinash Atreya, Hanjie Chen, Vicente Ordonez
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: retrieval-augmented, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
multiSourceItems.description: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an an
multiSourceItems.summary: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an animation problem. Inspired by computer animation, Mana employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline that transforms procedurally-generated grasp keyframes into manipulation trajectories through motion planning and reinforcement learning. The data generation process is largely automatic, requiring only a few mouse clicks to specify functional affordances (<1 minute per tool). Across four articulated tools spanning different scales and joint types, Mana achieves zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for both grasping and in-hand manipulation, demonstrating a scalable approach to dexterous articulated tool use.
multiSourceItems.author: Zhao-Heng Yin, Guanya Shi, Pieter Abbeel, C. Karen Liu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: tool use

multiSourceItems.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
multiSourceItems.description: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. multiSourceItems.summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
multiSourceItems.author: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen

multiSourceItems.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
multiSourceItems.description: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the origina
multiSourceItems.summary: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis. For 7 studies, the LLM could not produce a viable effect size estimate. For the remaining studies, our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a +/-0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases, where conclusions indicate whether the reanalysis supports the original claim. For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases. Together, these results show that LLMs can serve as a scalable tool for automated reproducibility assessment and provide a foundation for systematic auditing of empirical results in the social and behavioral sciences.
multiSourceItems.author: Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo, Anna Steinberg Schulten, Felix Henninger, Stefan Rose, Sarah Ball, Bolei Ma, Frauke Kreuter, Markus Weinmann, Stefan Feuerriegel
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

multiSourceItems.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
multiSourceItems.description: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge
multiSourceItems.summary: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.
multiSourceItems.author: Zongsheng Cao, Bihao Zhan, Jinxin Shi, Jiong Wang, Fangchen Yu, Zhijie Zhong, Zijie Guo, Tianshuo Peng, Zhuo Liu, Yi Xie, Xiang Zhuang, Yue Fan, Runmin Ma, Shiyang Feng, Xiangchao Yan, Anran Liu, Peng Ye, Wenlong Zhang, Shufei Zhang, Chunfeng Song, Fenghua Ling, Jie Zhou, Liang He, Bo Zhang, Lei Bai

multiSourceItems.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
multiSourceItems.description: With the growth of LLMs' (Large Language Models) capabilities, there has been an increasing push to curate high quality datasets by filtering samples in the training data. In general, Data Attribution (DA) methods aim to estimate how individual samples in a training dataset can precondition a model to generate certain outputs. As an example, one might be interested in which samples in the data could be the source of toxic behavior after training the LLM. Many methods quantify this conditioning t
multiSourceItems.summary: With the growth of LLMs' (Large Language Models) capabilities, there has been an increasing push to curate high quality datasets by filtering samples in the training data. In general, Data Attribution (DA) methods aim to estimate how individual samples in a training dataset can precondition a model to generate certain outputs. As an example, one might be interested in which samples in the data could be the source of toxic behavior after training the LLM. Many methods quantify this conditioning through the paradigm of influence functions. While methods of this family are effective in its function, they lack the necessary processing speed and storage compactness to be practically implemented on large datasets. We propose a method, Influcoder, as a quick and cost-effective approach to influence-based Data Attribution at scale.
multiSourceItems.author: Dimitri Kachler, Damien Sileo, Pascal Denis
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag

multiSourceItems.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
multiSourceItems.description: Tool-augmented LLM agents commonly rely on step-wise atomic tool calls, where each invocation, observation, and value transfer is exposed in the main reasoning trace. This creates an \emph{execution-granularity mismatch}: locally deterministic tool workflows are unfolded into repeated model-visible decisions, consuming context and forcing the model to manage low-level dataflow in the trace. We introduce \textbf{HyperTool}, a unified executable MCP-style tool interface that changes the model-visi
multiSourceItems.summary: Tool-augmented LLM agents commonly rely on step-wise atomic tool calls, where each invocation, observation, and value transfer is exposed in the main reasoning trace. This creates an \emph{execution-granularity mismatch}: locally deterministic tool workflows are unfolded into repeated model-visible decisions, consuming context and forcing the model to manage low-level dataflow in the trace. We introduce \textbf{HyperTool}, a unified executable MCP-style tool interface that changes the model-visible unit of tool execution. A model invokes HyperTool with a code block that can call existing tools through their original schemas, manipulate returned values, and pass intermediate results locally, folding deterministic tool subroutines into a single outer call. To train models to use this interface, we synthesize HyperTool-format trajectories from cross-tool compositional tasks and verify them in real MCP environments. On MCP-Universe, HyperTool improves average accuracy from 15.69\% to 35.29\% on Qwen3-32B and from 9.93\% to 33.33\% on Qwen3-8B, and surpass GPT-OSS and Kimi-k2.5 on average accuracy, showing that our HyperTool can substantially improve multi-step tool use.
multiSourceItems.author: Yaxin Du, Yifan Zhou, Yujie Ge, Jiajun Wang, Xianghe Pang, Shuo Tang, Tuney Zheng, Bryan Dai, Jian Yang, Siheng Chen
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, tool use, mcp

multiSourceItems.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
multiSourceItems.description: LLM-based agents have shown increasing potential in automating scientific discovery. Given an optimizable metric and an execution environment, they can propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, and have produced results that outperform human-designed approaches. As model capabilities continue to improve, we argue that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from prescribing agent workflows to designing agent environments: the resources, constraints, and interfac
multiSourceItems.summary: LLM-based agents have shown increasing potential in automating scientific discovery. Given an optimizable metric and an execution environment, they can propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, and have produced results that outperform human-designed approaches. As model capabilities continue to improve, we argue that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from prescribing agent workflows to designing agent environments: the resources, constraints, and interfaces that shape agent behavior. We frame this as environment engineering: building environments that amplify productive behaviors, such as open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight. We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery. EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention. EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple mathematics, kernel engineering, and machine learning tasks, including new state-of-the-art 26-circle packing results discovered with less than $11 in total API cost. We open-source our code and results, and call for environment engineering as a core research direction for developing reliable autonomous research agents.
multiSourceItems.author: Amy Xin, Jiening Siow, Junjie Wang, Zijun Yao, Fanjin Zhang, Jian Song, Lei Hou, Juanzi Li
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, evaluation, workflow

multiSourceItems.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
multiSourceItems.description: Detecting LLM reasoning failures at inference time without ground-truth labels has motivated a wide range of confidence baselines, including self-consistency, semantic entropy, and P(True), built on within-question sampling and self-evaluation. Operad theory, the formalism for systems built by iterated substitution, suggests a complementary diagnostic: a model's direct answer to a compositional query should agree with the answer it produces by composing a stated decomposition of the same query.
multiSourceItems.summary: Detecting LLM reasoning failures at inference time without ground-truth labels has motivated a wide range of confidence baselines, including self-consistency, semantic entropy, and P(True), built on within-question sampling and self-evaluation. Operad theory, the formalism for systems built by iterated substitution, suggests a complementary diagnostic: a model's direct answer to a compositional query should agree with the answer it produces by composing a stated decomposition of the same query. We instantiate this idea as operadic consistency (OC), a per-question signal. Across twelve instruction-tuned LLMs (4B to 671B parameters, open-weights and closed-source) on four multi-hop QA datasets, OC is strongly correlated with accuracy on every dataset (Pearson $r \in [0.86, 0.94]$, all $p \leq 0.0004$), and is the only signal we evaluate with $r \geq 0.85$ uniformly across all four datasets. Chain-of-thought self-consistency (CoT-SC; Wang et al., 2023) matches OC on HotpotQA and DROP ($r = 0.93, 0.87$) but drops to $r \approx 0.45$ on MuSiQue and StrategyQA. At the per-question level, OC contributes information beyond CoT-SC and semantic entropy on every dataset (cluster-robust $p \leq 10^{-16}$ for the OC coefficient), and the conclusion is robust to additionally controlling for constructed decomposition-aware baselines ($p \leq 10^{-13}$). The same signal yields selective-prediction improvements (accuracy at fixed coverage) over a tuned CoT-SC baseline at the equal-cost $K = 3$ budget (AUARC lifts of +0.086 to +0.096 and AUROC lifts of +0.092 to +0.164; 95% CIs exclude zero on every cell). On five frontier thinking models, where the decomposition is extracted from the model's own chain of thought, the same equal-cost comparison gives positive selective-prediction point-estimate lift on all 16 (dataset, budget, metric) cells tested, with 95% CIs excluding zero on 12 of the 16.
multiSourceItems.author: Nathaniel Bottman, Yinhong Liu, Kyle Richardson
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, rag, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
multiSourceItems.description: We introduce SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark for Slovak, a low-resource West Slavic language, comprising 31 datasets across 7 task types -- nearly 4$\times$ the depth of existing multilingual benchmark coverage for Slovak. Our evaluation of 31 embedding models reveals that large instruction-tuned multilingual models achieve the strongest performance, while existing Slovak-specific models trained for NLU tasks transfer poorly to embedding tasks. To address the
multiSourceItems.summary: We introduce SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark for Slovak, a low-resource West Slavic language, comprising 31 datasets across 7 task types -- nearly 4$\times$ the depth of existing multilingual benchmark coverage for Slovak. Our evaluation of 31 embedding models reveals that large instruction-tuned multilingual models achieve the strongest performance, while existing Slovak-specific models trained for NLU tasks transfer poorly to embedding tasks. To address the need for efficient, locally-deployable Slovak embeddings, we develop \texttt{e5-sk-small} (45M parameters) and \texttt{e5-sk-large} (365M) by applying vocabulary trimming and fine-tuning to Multilingual E5 models. Despite size reductions of up to 62\%, our open-source models achieve competitive performance with proprietary APIs while remaining locally deployable for semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). We release the benchmark, models, datasets, and code openly, hoping our approach offers a replicable path for other under-resourced languages.
multiSourceItems.author: Marek Šuppa, Andrej Ridzik, Daniel Hládek, Natália Kňažeková, Viktória Ondrejová
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: retrieval-augmented, rag, embedding, fine-tuning, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
multiSourceItems.description: Recursive language models (RLMs) showed that recursion over model calls is an effective strategy for long-context reasoning, and production coding agents have begun to write code that spawns subagents at scale, most recently in Anthropic's dynamic workflows. We name and study the pattern between these two lines of work, where the recursive unit is a full agent harness with filesystem tools, code execution, and planning rather than a model call with no tools. We call this the Recursive Agent Harn
multiSourceItems.summary: Recursive language models (RLMs) showed that recursion over model calls is an effective strategy for long-context reasoning, and production coding agents have begun to write code that spawns subagents at scale, most recently in Anthropic's dynamic workflows. We name and study the pattern between these two lines of work, where the recursive unit is a full agent harness with filesystem tools, code execution, and planning rather than a model call with no tools. We call this the Recursive Agent Harness (RAH) and frame it as harness recursion, the code-first extension to the model recursion of RLMs. A parent agent generates and runs an executable script that spawns subagent harnesses in parallel for fine-grained workloads and uses structured function calls for small subtasks. We provide a controlled evaluation on long-context reasoning. With the backbone held fixed at GPT-5 to match the published Codex and RLM baselines, RAH improves the Codex coding-agent baseline from 71.75% to 81.36% on Oolong-Synthetic (199 samples, 13 context-length buckets up to 4M tokens), a gain attributable to the harness rather than the model. With a stronger backbone, Claude Sonnet 4.5, the same design reaches 89.77%.
multiSourceItems.author: Elias Lumer, Sahil Sen, Kevin Paul, Vamse Kumar Subbiah
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, coding agent, evaluation, workflow

multiSourceItems.title: Tuning Agent-Based Predator-Prey Models Toward Lotka-Volterra Dynamics
multiSourceItems.description: Recent growth in compute power has made it increasingly feasible to use large-scale agent-based models to simulate complex adaptive systems. A central difficulty is that such models contain many local rules and parameters, where small changes can lead to runaway behaviour, population collapse, or saturation at artificial bounds. We study this problem in a continuous predator-prey system where sheep and wolves are active agents with local sensing, internal energy, and recurrent neural network-bas
multiSourceItems.summary: Recent growth in compute power has made it increasingly feasible to use large-scale agent-based models to simulate complex adaptive systems. A central difficulty is that such models contain many local rules and parameters, where small changes can lead to runaway behaviour, population collapse, or saturation at artificial bounds. We study this problem in a continuous predator-prey system where sheep and wolves are active agents with local sensing, internal energy, and recurrent neural network-based controllers. We ask whether environmental and demographic parameters can be tuned so that the resulting population dynamics resemble classical Lotka-Volterra cycles. We optimise these parameters with a feature-based loss that rewards sustained oscillations, phase lag, bounded populations, and long-term persistence, first for random controllers and then for evolved controllers in a more naturalistic setting. The model is implemented in ABMax, a JAX-based agent-based modelling framework that enables efficient batched simulation on hardware accelerators.
multiSourceItems.author: Corinna Mandl, Siddharth Chaturvedi, Marcel van Gerven
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent

multiSourceItems.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
multiSourceItems.description: Catastrophic forgetting is often viewed as the destruction of previously learned knowledge during sequential learning. Building on the Accessibility Collapse framework, we investigate the geometric structure of recoverability in continual learning. Using Split CIFAR-100 and a sequentially trained ResNet-18, we analyze recoverability, representational drift, and recovery complexity across ten tasks. We introduce Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), a measure of the minimum number of singular d
multiSourceItems.summary: Catastrophic forgetting is often viewed as the destruction of previously learned knowledge during sequential learning. Building on the Accessibility Collapse framework, we investigate the geometric structure of recoverability in continual learning. Using Split CIFAR-100 and a sequentially trained ResNet-18, we analyze recoverability, representational drift, and recovery complexity across ten tasks. We introduce Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), a measure of the minimum number of singular directions required to preserve 90 percent of full probe performance. Contrary to our Recoverability Diffusion hypothesis, recovery dimensionality remains stable throughout training (mean k_t = 8.0) despite substantial representational drift. Principal-angle drift strongly predicts recoverability (r = -0.862), and a simple geometric model explains 82.2 percent of recoverability variance. These findings support the Stable Recovery Manifold hypothesis, suggesting that forgotten knowledge remains compactly decodable despite representational reorganization. The results indicate that catastrophic forgetting is primarily an accessibility and manifold-alignment problem rather than information destruction.
multiSourceItems.author: Ayushman Trivedi, Bhavika Melwani
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: alignment

multiSourceItems.title: Operads for compositional reasoning in LLMs
multiSourceItems.description: Question decomposition, i.e. breaking a complex query into simpler sub-queries whose answers are composed to produce a final answer, is a widely used strategy for improving LLM reasoning, yet it currently lacks a rigorous mathematical foundation. In this paper, we propose operads, mathematical structures that model many-in, one-out operations and compositions thereof, as a natural framework for describing question decomposition. We define the questions operad $Q$, in which operations correspond
multiSourceItems.summary: Question decomposition, i.e. breaking a complex query into simpler sub-queries whose answers are composed to produce a final answer, is a widely used strategy for improving LLM reasoning, yet it currently lacks a rigorous mathematical foundation. In this paper, we propose operads, mathematical structures that model many-in, one-out operations and compositions thereof, as a natural framework for describing question decomposition. We define the questions operad $Q$, in which operations correspond to question templates and composition corresponds to substitution of sub-answers, and show how QA models can be interpreted as algebras over $Q$. Beyond reframing existing practice, this operadic perspective points toward new methods, in particular a notion of operadic consistency, which measures whether a QA model's answers agree across the partial collapses of a question decomposition tree. Empirical evaluation of operadic consistency is reported in our companion paper (Bottman, Liu, and Richardson, 2026), which finds it strongly correlated with accuracy across twelve LLMs and four multi-hop QA datasets and outperforming standard temperature-based self-consistency baselines. We argue that operads are the natural mathematical home for question decomposition, and that invariants such as operadic consistency open new directions for analyzing and improving the reliability of multi-step reasoning.
multiSourceItems.author: Nathaniel Bottman, Kyle Richardson
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: From Tokens to Faces: Investigating Discrete Speech Representations for 3D Facial Animation
multiSourceItems.description: The choice of speech representation is critical in speech-driven 3D facial animation. Representations differ in what they encode: SSL features emphasize segmental and semantic cues, neural codecs yield latents optimized for acoustic reconstruction, and ASR-style objectives produce label-based spaces. We evaluate four speech representation families for 3D facial synthesis, comparing their facial reconstruction quality across two facial decoders using objective metrics and a perceptual evaluation.
multiSourceItems.summary: The choice of speech representation is critical in speech-driven 3D facial animation. Representations differ in what they encode: SSL features emphasize segmental and semantic cues, neural codecs yield latents optimized for acoustic reconstruction, and ASR-style objectives produce label-based spaces. We evaluate four speech representation families for 3D facial synthesis, comparing their facial reconstruction quality across two facial decoders using objective metrics and a perceptual evaluation. We additionally conduct probing analyses that relate tokenized representations to phonetic units and to articulatory deformations. We found that encoding phonetic classes is beneficial for accurate facial animation prediction on both semantic and label-based representations with comparable facial animation quality. From the latter, we introduce an Audio Visual Text-to-Speech (AVTTS) pipeline that leverages, as a shared space, discrete representations to decode speech and 3D facial motion.
multiSourceItems.author: Pedro Correa, Olivier Perrotin, Samir Sadok, Paula Costa, Thomas Hueber
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, evaluation

normalizedKeys: title:kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 kimi k2 7 code

items.title: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code
items.summary: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code。相比 K2.6，其在 Kimi Code Bench v2 上提升 +21.8%，Program Bench 提升 +11.0%，MLS Bench Lite 提升 +31.5%。推理效率改进，推理 token 使用量降低 30%，长时编码任务中指令遵循和端到端成功率均提升。6x 高速模式即将推出，即日起可通过 Kimi API 和 Kimi Code 使用。

items.title: 小互开源公众号自动排版技能组合
items.summary: 小互（@xiaohu）升级了公众号排版技能组合，实现一句话完成排版、封面生成并一键发送到公众号草稿箱。该工具已开源，提供20种主题颜色可选，可自动分析内容进行排版，支持非Markdown文件。用户只需在Claude Code、Codex或OpenClaw中提供文章链接或文档位置，即可获得可视化预览界面进行选择，全程无需手动操作。

items.title: qiaomu-ai-prd：面向AI的PRD生成Prompt
items.summary: 推文提出AI Agent开发中人类与AI对PRD的需求不同，为此发布了一个专门服务于AI的PRD文档生成Prompt（命名为qiaomu-ai-prd）。开发者先使用该Prompt生成文档，再交给AI开发，可显著提升功能完整度和丰富性。安装指令为：`npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-ai-prd`，开源地址及Prompt见评论区。

trendEntities.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
normalizedKeys: title:苹果 ios 27 健康 app 大改 卡片布局 营养识别 围绝经期追踪

items.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
items.summary: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。
items.description: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。

trendEntities.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
normalizedKeys: title:spec 驱动开发 sdd 的三个 skills 覆盖 spec implement verify 闭环

items.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
items.summary: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。

items.summary: 为 Chrome 和 Codex 内置浏览器引入开发者模式。 Codex 可以使用 Chrome DevTools 协议（CDP）来调试浏览器问题，通过分析 JavaScript 性能、检查控制台输出、网络流量和页面状态。

items.title: 全自主无人机首次击毙了人类士兵
items.summary: 据《新科学家》6月10日报道，全自主无人机首次击毙了人类士兵。这是有记录以来第一次由完全自主运行的无人机执行致命攻击，标志着自主武器系统在实战中的新进展。该报道来自《新科学家》网站，目前尚无更多细节公布。

normalizedKeys: title:replit agent 新增自定义指令与技能功能

items.title: Replit Agent 新增自定义指令与技能功能
items.summary: AI 智能体很强大，但它们不记得你的偏好。 所以你总是重复指令--如何组织项目、你的品牌指南。 现在你可以通过自定义指令和技能让 Replit Agent 学会你的惯例。 它会在每个项目中自动将这些考虑进去。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic与dxc达成全球联盟 将claude引入关键行业系统

items.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
items.summary: Anthropic与IT服务公司DXC Technology达成多年全球联盟。DXC将培训数万名获得Claude认证的前沿部署工程师（FDE），将Claude引入其为全球大型银行、航空公司、保险公司及政府机构运营的关键系统。内部部署中，Claude已成为DXC OASIS平台的默认基础模型，该平台超95%代码由Claude编写，开发速度提升10倍，已服务50多家客户。DXC加入Claude Partner Network，将在保险、现代化服务、网络安全及应用服务四个领域率先推出基于Claude的解决方案。

trendEntities.title: Prometheus 融资120亿美元，估值410亿美元，定位"人工通用工程师"
normalizedKeys: title:prometheus 融资120亿美元 估值410亿美元 定位 人工通用工程师

items.title: Prometheus 融资120亿美元，估值410亿美元，定位"人工通用工程师"
items.summary: 杰夫·贝佐斯旗下AI公司Prometheus在成立仅7个月、尚无任何产品交付的情况下，以410亿美元估值完成120亿美元融资（最初估值62亿美元）。该公司定位为"人工通用工程师"，目标是将设计到制造的循环压缩10倍以上。但物理经济无法像互联网数据那样抓取，缺乏制造业训练数据。为此，Prometheus计划斥资1000亿美元收购传统工业企业，通过获取工厂生成的数据构建护城河。

items.title: Codex Goal指令生成Skill发布：一句话需求转目标
items.summary: 针对如何给Codex写Goal指令的问题，作者发布了一个Skill，可将一句话需求自动转化为目标，实现"睡前写指令、模型自动开发、第二天收菜"。安装命令：`npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-goal-meta-skill`。源码免费开源（见评论区），旨在简化4w字文档的阅读负担。

trendEntities.title: Runway与Lionsgate扩大战略合作

items.title: Runway与Lionsgate扩大战略合作
items.summary: 全球内容巨头Lionsgate与生成式AI研究公司Runway今日宣布扩大战略合作。Lionsgate已取得Runway股权，双方将启动联合开发项目，共同创作并制作新IP，首推一部基于Lionsgate现有IP和Runway生成模型的短剧系列。此外，Lionsgate将作为主持伙伴参与6月举行的Runway AI电影节。这是双方继2024年9月首次合作后的深化，Lionsgate此前已在预可视化、故事板及最终帧制作中应用Runway工具。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 启动 Claude Corps 全国奖学金项目
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 启动 claude corps 全国奖学金项目

items.title: Anthropic 启动 Claude Corps 全国奖学金项目
items.summary: Anthropic 推出 Claude Corps 奖学金项目，面向早期职业生涯的年轻人，培训 1，000 名研究员使用 Claude，并匹配给美国非营利组织全职工作一年。研究员年薪 85，000 美元及福利，每周接受 5 小时持续培训。Anthropic 初始投入 1.5 亿美元，合作方为 CodePath 和 Social Finance。至少 400 家非营利组织将参与接待，包括 Braven、Code the Dream、Heartland Forward 等。

trendEntities.title: Cursor 推出 Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
normalizedKeys: title:cursor 推出 auto review 机制 用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限

items.title: Cursor 推出 Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
items.summary: Cursor 近日推出 Auto-review，通过一个专门的分类器智能体在工具调用前审查动作风险。该分类器根据上下文判断动作是否与用户意图一致，高风险时阻止并返回解释给父智能体，低风险时放行。分类器采用小模型，运行在智能体循环内以避免额外延迟，并能读取工作区文件辅助判断。测试基于约12小时内部开发会话生成的6122条标签数据，以及针对读取密钥、操作生产数据等危险场景的合成数据。设计目标是在不频繁阻断日常开发的前提下，拦截风险动作。

trendEntities.title: 阿里云发布 Meoo CLI：本地 AI 编程项目可一键部署上线
normalizedKeys: title:阿里云发布 meoo cli 本地 ai 编程项目可一键部署上线

items.title: 阿里云发布 Meoo CLI：本地 AI 编程项目可一键部署上线
items.summary: 阿里云今日推出 Meoo（秒悟）开源命令行工具 Meoo CLI，面向开发者，支持 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor 等本地 AI 编程助手。通过调用阿里云云端能力，可自动完成数据库接入、用户登录、文件存储及项目发布，将本地项目一键部署上线并生成可分享的访问链接。该工具定位为本地 Agent 与秒悟云端能力的连接入口，已开放下载，支持主流 Linux、macOS 及 Windows 平台。

trendEntities.title: 千问推出首个足球预测AI助手，竞猜赢奖并捐建球场
normalizedKeys: title:千问推出首个足球预测ai助手 竞猜赢奖并捐建球场

items.title: 千问推出首个足球预测AI助手，竞猜赢奖并捐建球场
items.summary: 千问上线首个足球预测AI助手，基于海量大数据（含历史比赛、球员数据、伤病、美加墨地貌及天气等）。以6月22日挪威对塞内加尔为例，预测"1：1平局"，理由为气候差异。活动：参与全部104场竞猜，预测超80场且准确率超千问可抽万元大奖（100个名额）；预测超32场可抽千问AI眼镜G1（1000副），该眼镜支持赛后分析、拍屏识球员及赛事结果订阅。累积积分将向乡村学校捐建足球场，目标至少50所。

EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
normalizedKeys: title:evoarena tracking memory evolution for robust llm agents in dynamic environments

items.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
items.description: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and so
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.
items.author: Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li, Jiaying Wu, Yihuai Lan, Shuyue Stella Li, Huichi Zhou, Bowen Jiang, Lei Wang, Jun Wang, Anh Tuan Luu, Caiming Xiong, Hae Won Park, Bryan Hooi, Zhiyuan Hu

trendEntities.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
normalizedKeys: title:learning to reason by analogy via retrieval augmented reinforcement fine tuning

items.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
items.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework
items.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework that teaches language models to reason by analogy. RA-RFT uses gold-relevance distillation to train a retriever that ranks contexts by expected reasoning benefit rather than semantic overlap, and then fine-tunes the policy model via reinforcement fine-tuning methods with retrieved analogous demonstrations, so the model learns to leverage reasoning traces under verifiable outcome rewards. We further analyze the diversity of retrieved contexts and find that reasoning-aware retrieval surfaces complementary solution strategies that provide distinct reasoning scaffolds for individual problems. Across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks, RA-RFT consistently outperforms standard reinforcement fine-tuning methods. For example, it improves AIME 2025 average@32 accuracy by 7.1 and 2.8 points over GRPO for Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B respectively -- suggesting that reasoning-aware retrieval is a complementary axis of improvement and orthogonal to advances in reward design or training curricula.
items.author: Zilin Xiao, Qi Ma, Chun-cheng Jason Chen, Xintao Chen, Avinash Atreya, Hanjie Chen, Vicente Ordonez
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: retrieval-augmented, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

trendEntities.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
normalizedKeys: title:mana dexterous manipulation of articulated tools

items.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
items.description: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. items.summary: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an animation problem. Inspired by computer animation, Mana employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline that transforms procedurally-generated grasp keyframes into manipulation trajectories through motion planning and reinforcement learning. The data generation process is largely automatic, requiring only a few mouse clicks to specify functional affordances (<1 minute per tool). Across four articulated tools spanning different scales and joint types, Mana achieves zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for both grasping and in-hand manipulation, demonstrating a scalable approach to dexterous articulated tool use.
items.author: Zhao-Heng Yin, Guanya Shi, Pieter Abbeel, C. Karen Liu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: tool use

items.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
items.description: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. items.summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
items.author: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen

trendEntities.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
normalizedKeys: title:automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models

items.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
items.description: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the origina
items.summary: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis. For 7 studies, the LLM could not produce a viable effect size estimate. For the remaining studies, our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a +/-0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases, where conclusions indicate whether the reanalysis supports the original claim. For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases. Together, these results show that LLMs can serve as a scalable tool for automated reproducibility assessment and provide a foundation for systematic auditing of empirical results in the social and behavioral sciences.
items.author: Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo, Anna Steinberg Schulten, Felix Henninger, Stefan Rose, Sarah Ball, Bolei Ma, Frauke Kreuter, Markus Weinmann, Stefan Feuerriegel
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

trendEntities.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
normalizedKeys: title:agents k1 towards agent native knowledge orchestration

items.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
items.description: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge
items.summary: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.

sourceItems.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
sourceItems.description: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge
sourceItems.summary: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.
